mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in FEDFUNDS reflects a Fed tightening cycle that raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 20-day forward returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or forward returns are not significantly negative during rising-rate periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window from a cyclical low, it signals an inflection toward labor-market deterioration that precedes broader earnings weakness, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of historical instances where UNRATE rose >0.3pp in 3 months, 60-day forward SPX returns were positive, or the effect is not statistically distinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth (RSAFS) combined with a stable or falling FEDFUNDS rate indicates robust consumer demand without offsetting monetary restraint, jointly signaling accelerating economic momentum that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month. Neither series alone is sufficient; the effect requires their joint configuration.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 4% while FEDFUNDS change over 3 months <= 0
failure_condition: Interaction-triggered instances show 20-day forward returns no better than the unconditional base rate (difference in means not significant at p > 0.05), or single-series RSAFS momentum alone explains equivalent predictive power (interaction term insignificant in regression).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, decelerating INDPRO growth signals approaching capacity constraints and margin compression for industrial firms, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter; in early or mid-cycle regimes the same deceleration is a benign pause and carries no predictive signal.
failure_condition: During periods independently classified as late-cycle, INDPRO deceleration fails to precede negative 60-day SPX returns more often than a 50% base rate, or the relationship reverses sign versus early/mid-cycle subsamples with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in PAYEMS growth reflects improving labor demand and consumer income prospects, generating a short-lived positive sentiment tailwind that lifts S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS month-over-month change and 5-day forward returns stays below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST of more than 5% over a trailing 3-month period signals weakening housing demand, a leading indicator of broader consumer retrenchment, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month as markets price in slower growth.
failure_condition: Triggered HOUST declines are followed by positive or flat 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of historical instances, invalidating the directional claim.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS rises while CPIAUCSL inflation growth simultaneously decelerates, the resulting increase in ex-ante real interest rates tightens financial conditions more than nominal rates alone suggest, jointly compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter. Neither the nominal rate nor inflation alone captures this real-rate tightening effect.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0 while CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth decelerates
failure_condition: The joint real-rate-rising signal fails to produce significantly more negative 60-day returns than FEDFUNDS-only signals (interaction coefficient insignificant at p > 0.05 in regression), or effect direction reverses in more than 40% of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion with UNRATE already near multi-decade lows (below 4.5%), further declines in UNRATE combined with continued PAYEMS gains signal an overheating labor market that raises wage-inflation and rate-hike risk, causing negative short-horizon S&P 500 returns as markets price faster tightening; in early-cycle regimes with slack labor markets the same signal is positive.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.5% and falling, with PAYEMS month-over-month gain positive
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: During late-cycle low-unemployment periods, the joint UNRATE-PAYEMS tightening signal is not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in at least 55% of instances, or the sign flips relative to early-cycle subsamples with p < 0.05, disconfirming regime conditionality.
